demographics.living_situation: lives at home; an adult child is present and made the after-hours call

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The pain hit me about an hour after eating a burger, it is gripping my ribs and shooting straight up into my right shoulder. I can't sit still and I've been pacing the floor. I threw up once and even paracetamol did nothing.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The pain came on suddenly after a heavy fatty dinner and it's so bad I can't rest or keep anything down; I've had milder versions before but never this bad.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 45 minutes to an hour after eating a burger and chips this evening; started roughly 2 hours before this call
symptom_narrative.duration: a couple of hours so far and not letting up
symptom_narrative.character: a severe gripping, cramping pain that comes in waves; when it peaks I can't stay still
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst I've had — I keep pacing because I can't get comfortable in any position
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: high up on the right side under my ribs, and it shoots up into my right shoulder blade
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: comes in waves but hasn't fully settled since it started
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far — I took paracetamol about an hour ago and it did absolutely nothing
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it came on after a fatty meal; moving around doesn't really change it
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: really nauseated and I vomited once; no chest pain, no trouble breathing
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had similar but much milder attacks before that went away on their own after 3 or 4 hours
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: can't sit or lie still, can't eat or drink, can't rest

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: paracetamol (took some about an hour ago for this pain)

social_history_volunteered.diet_and_exercise: ate a burger and chips tonight; says takeaway/fast food is fairly regular
social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: at home this evening with an adult child present

objective_data_offered.value: patient is pacing and restless, unable to sit or lie still, looks sweaty and in obvious distress, holding the upper right side of the abdomen
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: appearance over video only; no clinician examination performed

objective_data_offered.value: the whites of the eyes and the skin look normal colour on video — no obvious yellowing
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: video lighting is imperfect; not a substitute for a clinical jaundice assessment

objective_data_offered.value: adult child reports one episode of vomiting (food/fluid, not blood), that the patient cannot keep any food or drink down because of the pain, and has not passed any black or bloody stool
patient reports feeling hot/cold is not a feature — no shivering or chills, and does not feel feverish
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-reported; no measured temperature available at home

objective_data_offered.value: fast-food wrappers on the kitchen counter from the evening meal
